food_subgroup: Toasted sub sandwich with processed meat, cheese, and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic submarine sandwich class (FOODON_00002473); no specific Subway Melt variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKR covers sandwiches broadly; no Subway-brand specific code exists. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for a Subway Melt sandwich product. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard six-inch Subway Melt sub, approximately 250 g, served ala carte without a drink or side.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Melt 6-inch sub — USDA Branded Food / Subway Nutrition Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Subway Melt 6-inch sub (FDC entries for Subway brand products, e.g. FDC ID 2103697 and related entries). Values are expressed per 100 g by back-calculation from the 250 g serving size reported. The Singapore ala carte variant is assumed to use the same standard Subway Melt formulation. Minor regional sauce variations may affect sodium and fat slightly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based mixed sandwiches — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white/wheat sub bread and mixed-ingredient sandwiches
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Melt product. GI estimated at 45 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for wheat-based sub rolls (GI ~40–50) and mixed-ingredient toasted sandwiches. The protein and fat content from meat and cheese lower the effective GI relative to plain bread. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 62.0) / 100 = 27.9, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 62.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~1550 mg per 6-inch serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the Subway Melt due to its very high sodium load (~1550 mg per serving), which represents approximately 67% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; choosing low-sodium sauces and omitting processed meats can partially reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~27.9 per serving) from refined wheat bread base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the Subway Melt has a moderate-to-high glycemic load per serving (~27.9) driven by the refined wheat bread; opting for a wholegrain or multigrain bread variant, reducing portion size to a 6-inch sub, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from processed meats and cheese, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the Subway Melt presents a moderate concern due to its saturated fat content (~3.0 g per 100 g from bacon, ham, and American cheese) and high sodium (~620 mg per 100 g); limiting frequency of consumption and choosing leaner protein options with reduced-fat cheese can lower cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Melt is a toasted sub sandwich sold by the international fast-food chain Subway, available in Singapore as an ala carte item (without a drink or side). The standard Subway Melt is built on a six-inch or footlong Italian or wheat bread roll and filled with a combination of ham, turkey breast, and bacon, topped with American cheese slices that are melted under a toaster oven. Standard vegetable toppings include lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, cucumber, and olives, with optional sauces such as mayonnaise, mustard, or sweet onion. The sandwich is characterised by its warm, toasted texture, savoury and slightly smoky flavour from the bacon and melted cheese, and a soft bread interior with a lightly crisped crust. Nutritionally, the Subway Melt is a moderate-energy, protein-rich sandwich (~218 kcal per 100 g; ~13 g protein per 100 g) with a significant sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the processed meats and cheese. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI range) for the standard wheat bread base, though the glycemic load per full six-inch serving is moderate (~27.9). Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while the moderate glycemic load warrants portion awareness for those with Type 2 Diabetes. The Subway Melt is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without specific verification of the local outlet's halal status. In Singapore, Subway outlets may carry the Melt as part of their standard menu, and it is available on food delivery platforms including field survey.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Subway Melt?
Is Subway Melt halal in Singapore?
Subway Melt ala carte price Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Subway Melt 6-inch sub products. The Singapore ala carte variant is assumed to follow the same standard Subway Melt formulation; minor regional differences in sauce, bread, or portion size may introduce small deviations. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat sub bread entries, not a direct measurement of this product.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Foods source entries for this product. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Foods source entries for this product. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Foods source entries for this product. 4. The input food_group was listed as 'Vegetables' in the seed record, which is incorrect for this product; corrected to 'Mixed' reflecting the composite nature of the sandwich. 5. Halal status is not confirmed for all Singapore Subway outlets; consumers should verify with the specific outlet. 6. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct peer-reviewed GI measurement for the Subway Melt product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The Singapore ala carte price of SGD 10.10 is preserved from the field survey seed record. 8. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Western fast-food item sold in Singapore.
